title: Filtering, Three-Valued Logic, Patterns, Ordering, and Pagination · Query Trace: Three Valued Logic
summary: A queue view must include the correct unresolved tickets, handle missing owners, and remain deterministic across pages. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql three valued logic increment in a deterministic queue-filter query suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL.
Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 6, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL. A defensible solution applies Three Valued Logic, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a deterministic queue-filter query suite.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-three-valued-logic — Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL.
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 4;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-three-valued-logic-m0fr01
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id, owner_id = 1 AS owner_is_one
FROM tickets
WHERE owner_id = 1 OR owner_id IS NULL
ORDER BY ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
